title: jaubertia reboudiana
intro: Jaubertia reboudiana, also known as Khat or Catha edulis, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, its leaves being the most commonly used part for consumption and medicinal purposes. Jaubertia reboudiana has been found to possess stimulant and anxiogenic properties, attributed to the presence of cathinone, a psychoactive alkaloid. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including increased alertness, reduced fatigue, and improved cognitive function. The leaves can be prepared in different forms, such as chewing the fresh leaves, smoking the dried leaves, or drinking a tea made from the plant.
